title: The ן¿½Seaboltן¿½sן¿½ Volunteers
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ They towed the Seabolt down the stream,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And through the harbourן¿½s mouth;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ She spread her wings and sailed away
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To seek the sunny South.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But, ah! she met with storm on storm
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ere half her course had run;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And all her masts were torn away,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And all her boats save one.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The good old ship had settled far
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Beneath her cargo line,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Her riven sides were drinking deep
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The draughts of ocean brine.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ There gathered round the only boat
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The women pale with fear,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And trembling little ones, who clung
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To those who held them dear.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Then spoke the captain, brave and true,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ His voice rose oן¿½er the roar;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The boat will save us all but five,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ She cannot float with more!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And backward from the side he steppedן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ (He had been born at sea)
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Now who will seek in oceanן¿½s depths
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A sailorן¿½s grave with me?ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Then up there stepped a merchant stout,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ His face was brown and tan:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Iן¿½ll volunteer to stay on board,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For Iן¿½m an Englishman!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Then spoke a gallant gentleman,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A lover of romance:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Remain I for the ladiesן¿½ sake,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For Iן¿½m a son of France!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And next there spoke a Highlander:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Go search the wide world round,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Youן¿½ll find no spot where on the earth
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A Scotsman is not found!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And then there spoke a lad to whom
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Killarneyן¿½s lakes were dear:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½It wonן¿½t be said that Ireland found
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ No place of honour here!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The boat pushed from the vesselן¿½s side
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Amid the ringing cheers;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And now beneath Old Ocean sleep
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The Seaboltן¿½s volunteers.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Henry Lawson represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Henry Lawson's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
